title: KQED - 52 years ago, San Francisco's beachside amusement...
content: ## KQED's Post. ### **KQED**. On that sad day in 1972, feelings ran so high that legendary animatronic cackler Laughing Sal had her head stolen! So what happened to Sal's dome? And who had the last laugh? The Bay Area's NPR and PBS station. Loved it went there all the time ❤️. My dad and I drove from Santa Rosa on the very last day in '72. I remember Playland, I loved it. Our father loaded us and drove from Vacaville to a beach day and fun trip to the Fun House, the mirrored maze entry and to fun inside moving barrel, the slides, and of course the spinning wheel. Sometimes we just went to play Skee-ball or buy a fresh It's It. They also sold frozen chocolate covered bananas, I loved those too. She gave me nightmares, on quiet nights we could hear the seals barking near the Cliff House and I could swear I heard Sal laughing too!
